[[Joe Westerman]] | stadium = [[Wheldon Road]] | agg_attendance = {{formatnum:{{sum|10170|7040|9026||6996|7869|8269|7967|6965|7897|7331|7247|7449|9053}}}} | avg_attendance = {{formatnum:{{mean|10170|7040|9026||6996|7869|8269|7967|6965|7897|7331|7247|7449|9053|round=0}}}} | high_attendance = 10,170 | low_attendance = 6,965 | top_tries = [[Innes Senior]] (17) | top_goals = [[Rowan Milnes]] (51) | top_points = [[Rowan Milnes]] (135) | prev_season = [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023]] | next_season = [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Castleford Tigers seasons }} The '''2024 season''' was the '''[[Castleford Tigers]]'''' 99th season in the [[Rugby Football League]] and their 17th consecutive season in the top flight of [[Rugby league in England|English rugby league]]. The club competed in the [[2024 Super League season|2024 Super League]] and the [[2024 Challenge Cup]]. This was Castleford's first and only season under [[Craig Lingard]] as head coach. The team was jointly captained by [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]] and [[Joe Westerman]]. They finished the Super League season in 10th place. == Kits == '''Supplier:''' Oxen

'We've massively underperformed' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively |access-date=23 October 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} The head coach role had been left vacant after the expiry of [[Danny Ward (rugby league)|Danny Ward]]'s short-term deal.{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=2 October 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers coaching situation after Danny Ward rejects with another candidate putting hat in ring for Super League job |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-coaching-situation-after-danny-ward-rejects-with-another-candidate-putting-hat-in-ring-for-super-league-job/ |access-date=10 November 2024 |website=TotalRL}} On 17 October 2023, it was announced that former assistant [[Craig Lingard]] had been appointed, becoming the club's fourth permanent head coach within 12 months. [[Danny McGuire]] additionally joined from [[Hull KR]] as the new assistant coach.{{Cite web |date=17 October 2023 |title=Craig Lingard: Castleford Tigers appoint former Keighley and Batley boss as head coach |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67134691 |access-date=17 October 2023 |website=BBC Sport}} [[Joe Westerman]] was named co-captain alongside [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]].{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=11 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers coach Craig Lingard appoints co-captains for 2024: 'They know what it means to play for Cas' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-craig-lingard-appoints-co-captains-paul-mcshane-joe-westerman-for-2024 |access-date=10 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} In the offseason, the playing squad underwent a large turnover with a focus on reducing average age.{{Cite web |date=29 January 2024 |title=2024 Season Preview: Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3877/2024-season-preview-castleford-tigers |access-date=20 September 2024 |website=Super League}}{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=19 September 2023 |title='We want to bring the age profile of our squad down': Castleford Tigers director speaks out as club release experienced heads Suaia Matagi & Greg Eden |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/we-want-to-bring-the-age-profile-of-our-squad-down-castleford-tigers-director-speaks-out-as-club-release-experienced-heads-suaia-matagi-greg-eden |access-date=20 September 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} Departures included mainstays [[Nathan Massey (rugby league, born 1989)|Nathan Massey]] and [[Greg Eden]] along with senior players such as [[Gareth Widdop]], [[Niall Evalds]] and [[Jordan Turner]]. Under the new [[IMG Grading for the British Rugby Football League|IMG grading system]], league status would no longer be solely dependent on table position, so the team operated with a reduced playing budget as the club prioritised off-field improvements.{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=4 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers can take leaf out of rugby league rivals Wakefield Trinity's book says coach Craig Lingard |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-can-take-leaf-out-of-rugby-league-rivals-wakefield-trinitys-book-says-coach-craig-lingard-4579488 |access-date=20 September 2024 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}{{Cite web |last=Chapman-Coombe |first=Louis |date=18 September 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers recruitment update as Craig Lingard reveals budget change and 'movement' pledge |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-confident-for-movement-on-recruitment-in-coming-weeks-after-budget-increase |access-date=20 September 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} Accordingly, the Tigers were widely predicted to compete with newly promoted [[London Broncos|London]] for the '[[Wooden spoon (award)|wooden spoon]]'.{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=17 February 2024 |title=Craig Lingard hits back against media predictions of Castleford Tigers for Super League 2024 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/craig-lingard-hits-back-against-media-predictions-of-castleford-tigers-for-super-league-2024/ |access-date=10 November 2024 |website=TotalRL}}{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=9 February 2024 |title=Super League 2024: The Love Rugby League team predict the new season |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-2024-the-love-rugby-league-team-predict-the-new-season |access-date=10 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}{{Cite web |last=Schofield |first=Garry |date=3 January 2024 |title=The Garry Schofield Column: My prediction for Castleford Tigers in Super League 2024 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/the-garry-schofield-column-my-prediction-for-castleford-tigers-in-super-league-2024/ |access-date=10 November 2024 |website=TotalRL}} On the eve of the season, their proposals for an upgrade of [[Wheldon Road]] were approved by the council.{{Cite web |last=Moss |first=Alex |date=15 February 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers ground revamp and employment site given approval |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-leeds-68306467 |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=BBC News}} Castleford began the season poorly, falling to defeats against highly-rated [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the opening rounds. After a heavy loss to [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in round 4, Lingard said the team's capitulation was unacceptable and moved poor performers to the reserves.{{Cite web |last=Pattison |first=Cam |date=15 March 2024 |title="Go somewhere else" – Castleford Tigers boss issues brutal warning to players |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/go-somewhere-else-castleford-tigers-boss-issues-brutal-warning-to-players-89135/ |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}} The team showed improved resilience despite defeat in the following round at [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]],{{Cite web |date=16 March 2024 |title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 40-14 Castleford Tigers - Fourth win in five games for French side |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68569765 |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=BBC Sport}} and they saw off [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] to advance in the Challenge Cup. However, they were unable to break down [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in round 6, with first-half control not being converted into points by their blunt attack; this sixth consecutive league loss marked Castleford's worst start to a season since 2004.{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=28 March 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' 7 worst Super League starts analysed as class of 2024 make unwanted history |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-7-worst-super-league-starts-analysed-as-class-of-2024-make-unwanted-history |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} The Tigers secured their first league win of the year against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in round 7, with [[Innes Senior]] notably scoring four tries. However, with injury problems mounting and fifteen players unavailable, Castleford crashed out of the cup with a 54-point loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=11 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers coach reveals extent of squad decimation ahead of Challenge Cup quarter-final tie |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-reveals-extent-of-squad-decimation-ahead-of-challenge-cup-quarter-final-tie |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} In April, the club announced a host of midseason signings including [[Tex Hoy]], [[Louis Senior]] and [[Corey Hall (rugby league)|Corey Hall]]. Another league defeat to Wigan was followed by an impressive 40–0 win against [[London Broncos|London]], and a late comeback against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] secured a thrilling draw in round 10. Castleford's hit-and-miss performances continued over the following weeks, when they were trounced by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], won against table rivals [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] (with Hoy scoring the club's try of the season),{{Cite tweet |number=1833188619894591786 |user=CTRLFC |title=Your votes are in and counted and the TOTS is Tex Hoy against his former club Hull FC in round 12! |author=Castleford Tigers |author-link=Castleford Tigers}} and were comfortably beaten by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. June saw an upturn in the team's performances, with battling displays in consecutive games against the eventual top two, [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[Hull KR]] - only beaten by one penalty goal and one drop goal respectively. Centre [[Sam Wood (rugby league, born 1997)|Sam Wood]]'s impressive form earned him an {{Rlnt|England}} call up, but he suffered an injury on international duty that ruled him out for the season.{{Cite web |date=4 July 2024 |title=Castleford & England centre Wood out for season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4ngzm8eldro |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=BBC Sport}} In round 16, Castleford edged a low-scoring affair to earn an away win against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] for only the second time in 34 years.{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=6 July 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' incredible Jekyll and Hyde record at St Helens dissected after famous victory |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-incredible-jekyll-and-hyde-record-at-st-helens-dissected-after-famous-victory |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} They then duly beat bottom side [[London Broncos|London]], before the standout performance of the year against [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] secured a third straight victory with two tries from [[Liam Horne]].{{Cite web |date=6 November 2024 |title=2024 Season Review: Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4680/2024-season-review-castleford-tigers |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=Super League}}{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=21 July 2024 |title=6 conclusions from Castleford Tigers' win over Catalans Dragons |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/6-conclusions-from-castleford-tigers-win-over-catalans-dragons-alex-mellor-liam-horne-jacob-miller |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} Lingard said the team was "unrecognisable" from the start of the year, having surpassed their 2023 points tally by July.{{Cite web |last=Badwal |first=Sunny |date=21 July 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers 'unrecognisable' as they fend off Catalans Dragons with Tex Hoy try |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-unrecognisable-as-they-fend-off-catalans-dragons-with-tex-hoy-try-4710896 |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}} Their streak was brought to an end by two hard-fought defeats at the hands of play-off hopefuls [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]]. In round 21, Castleford sustained their heaviest defeat in three months in their visit to table-topping [[Hull KR]].{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=9 August 2024 |title=6 conclusions from Hull KR's win over Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/6-conclusions-from-hull-kr-win-over-castleford-tigers |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}} They went into their [[Magic Weekend]] fixture against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] just a point adrift of their 9th-placed opponents, however a meagre loss diminished hopes of moving up the table.{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=18 August 2024 |title=Report and reaction as Castleford Tigers end Magic Weekend with loss to Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/report-and-reaction-as-castleford-tigers-end-magic-weekend-with-loss-to-huddersfield-giants-4746909 |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} Lingard began to rotate the team in the remaining rounds, experimenting with different combinations and giving minutes to academy players like [[Jenson Windley]], [[Fletcher Rooney]] and [[Akim Matvejev]].{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=23 August 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' 'experimental' team for Warrington clash explored including half-back debut and key returnee |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-experimental-team-for-warrington-clash-explored-including-half-back-debut-and-key-returnee |access-date=13 to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] was followed by a high-scoring win against [[Hull F.C.|Hull]], where [[Rowan Milnes]] notched 19 points.{{Cite web |date=31 August 2024 |title=Six-try Castleford win at struggling Hull FC |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39k44r2p9yo |access-date=13 November 2024 |website=BBC Sport}} Castleford fell to substantial losses against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], and [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the final three rounds of the season, dampening the improved mood around the club, although their 10th-placed finish represented a marginal improvement on the previous campaign.{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=18 September 2024 |title=Craig Lingard rates Castleford Tigers season ahead
